req.title: Submit timesheet — variant 4
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The violation was in test[3]: 'test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80' had kind=negative but heuristic=BVA. Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4, BVA boundary tests that probe the just-outside-boundary invalid value (upper+1 = 81) are correctly classified as kind=boundary because the heuristic drives the test design — the input value is chosen specifically because it sits at the boundary. I have corrected kind from 'negative' to 'boundary' for that test. All other tests are preserved exactly as before.

tests.name: test_submit_valid_timesheet_happy_path
actions: POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceed_24
actions: Build a payload where Wednesday has 25 hours
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: Response body references per-day validation error
trace: R-WEB-070: error pathway — 422 when per-day hours outside 0-24

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_boundary_at_80
actions: Build a 7-day payload that sums exactly to 80 (e.g. 12+12+12+12+12+12+8)
trace: R-WEB-070: upper boundary of weekly total = 80 (inclusive)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80
actions: Build a 7-day payload that sums to 81 (e.g. 12+12+12+12+12+12+9)
expected: Response body references weekly-total validation error
trace: R-WEB-070: error pathway — 422 when weekly total outside 0-80 (81 = upper+1)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_per_day_lower_boundary_zero
actions: Build a payload where every day has 0 hours (total=0)
trace: R-WEB-070: lower boundary of per-day hours = 0 (inclusive)
R-WEB-070: lower boundary of weekly total = 0 (inclusive)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: A timesheet for the target week exists in state=draft
actions: Create or confirm a draft timesheet via <create_draft_timesheet> fixture
POST to /timesheets/submit with the draft ID and valid hours payload
expected: state transitioned from draft to submitted
trace: R-WEB-070: triggers — employee submits weekly timesheet

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_rate_limit_exceeded
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for the user is at or near its threshold
actions: Call <exhaust_rate_limit> helper to consume all allowed requests for the user
Attempt one additional POST to /timesheets/submit with a valid payload
expected: Response contains Retry-After header or rate-limit detail
trace: R-WEB-070: operation must be rate-limited per user
